entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The importance of transactions in the context of cryptocurrency economics was emphasized, highlighting that the primary advantage of using money over bartering is the reduction of transaction costs. It was noted that individuals who do not engage in transactions fail to realize any benefits from their assets. This notion extends to the security of blockchain technology, which heavily relies on transaction fees for maintaining censorship resistance. Unlike security against individual double-spending, which is a separate issue, overall chain security is vulnerable if there are no transactions.
Furthermore, the concept of hoarding cryptocurrencies without the intention of eventual expenditure was critically analyzed. It was argued that merely saving digital coins without transacting them leads to natural decay through opportunity costs. The necessity of transactions was underlined not only for investment purposes but also for realizing profits and earning interest. Hence, demand creation through saving eventually compels individuals toward transacting, which in turn supports both demand and security within the network. Without transactions, there would be no demand, ultimately compromising the security and functionality of the currency system.
